svg.title: What the research says — PLG growth at Series B
svg.desc: Four cited sources on why content velocity and community density — not lead generation — is the documented growth lever for B2B SaaS companies running a product-led growth model at Series B.
svg.text: What the research says about PLG growth at Series B
Four external sources, all 2024–2026. None of them say "build more outbound."
Year 4
Figma waited until year 4 to hire a sales team.
"Phase 3 was community-led growth. Free users brought
Figma into their teams, who brought it into their companies.
Sales as a function came later, in Phase 4 — after the
community-led motion had been undeniable for years."
— First Round Review, "The 5 Phases of Figma's
Community-Led Growth from Stealth to Enterprise"
is "good" free-to-paid conversion. 8–12% is "great."
"The companies that hit the upper end of this band are
almost always content-and-community-led. The conversion
happens not in the moment of the upgrade, but in the
months before — through density of customer stories,
visible workflow examples, and peer signal."
— Lenny's Newsletter, free-to-paid conversion benchmarks
The SaaS funding climate has shifted.
"Growth is no longer the defining challenge. Cost control,
retention, and AI governance are. Series B startups in
stricter capital environments are forced to optimize what
they have, not buy more leads. Retention has quietly
become a more reliable growth engine than acquisition."
— GrowthNavigate, B2B SaaS Statistics 2026
average MQL → SQL conversion across B2B SaaS.
"Even when leads do come in, 87% never convert into
qualified opportunities. The bottleneck for B2B SaaS in
2026 is rarely the volume of leads. It's the quality of
signal that makes a free user — or a procurement team —
decide the tool is worth committing to."
— SaaSrise, 2026 B2B SaaS Growth Program
The PLG playbook at Series B isn't "build more outbound." It's "build the content density that converts
free users in the months before the upgrade decision." That's the lever the agent attacks.
